query: Google Cloud Next '26 agenda keynote networking infrastructure announcements preview
answer:
Now I have comprehensive data. Let me compile my full research synthesis.

---

# Google Cloud Next '26: Agenda, Keynotes & Networking Infrastructure — Research Report

## 1. Event Overview

**Google Cloud Next '26** takes place **April 22–24, 2026** at the **Mandalay Bay Convention Center, Las Vegas**. A Partner Summit runs April 21–24 (one day before the main event). The Session Library launched February 24, 2026. The 2025 edition set attendance records; 2026 is designed to be larger, with an estimated ~30,000 attendees. Ticket prices start at $999.

---

## 2. Keynotes & Spotlights

### Opening Keynote: "The Agentic Cloud"
- **When:** Wednesday, April 22, 2026, 9:00 AM PT
- **Speaker:** **Thomas Kurian**, CEO of Google Cloud
- Thomas Kurian's confirmed sessions include:
  - *"Opening keynote: The agentic cloud"*
  - *"From vision to value: Powering the agentic enterprise, together"*
  - *"Fireside chat with Thomas Kurian"*
- The keynote is expected to feature announcements on custom silicon (potentially TPU v8), Gemini model updates, ADK 2.0, and the A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol.

### Developer Keynote
- **When:** Thursday, April 23, 2026 (morning)
- Focus on hands-on practitioner content, live demos of new APIs and IDEs.

### Infrastructure Spotlight
- **Speaker:** **Mark Lohmeyer**, VP & GM of AI and Computing Infrastructure
- **Topic:** The future of Google's AI and compute ecosystem (AI Hypercomputer, Compute Engine, etc.)

### Cross-Cloud Infrastructure Spotlight (Networking Focus)
- **Speakers:**
  - **Muninder Sambi**, VP & GM Networking
  - **Drew Bradstock**, Sr. Director, Compute and Kubernetes Orchestration
- **Topic:** Google's **agentic cross-cloud infrastructure** — this is the primary networking infrastructure spotlight.

---

## 3. Networking Infrastructure Sessions (Confirmed from Session Library)

The "Networking" topic filter is a dedicated category in the session library. Key networking sessions confirmed:

### BRK3-037 — Cross-Cloud Network: An intelligent, governed, performant fabric for global AI
- **Day:** April 23 (Day 2), 5:15–6:00 PM | Breakers E
- **Level:** Advanced Technical | Architecture, Networking
- **Description:** Introduces **new capabilities** for centralized governance and observability to connect agents, models, and tools. Covers **GKE Inference Gateway expansions** (eliminating compute silos by pooling traffic to accelerator capacity) and **AI-optimized VPC and GKE networking** engineered to eliminate bottlenecks in model training and inference.

### BRK3-038 — Build a cross-cloud network to connect apps securely across clouds
- **Day:** April 23 (Day 2), 10:30–11:15 AM | Mandalay Bay F
- **Level:** Advanced Technical | Architecture, Multicloud, Networking
- **Speaker:** **Himanshu Mehra**, Director PM, Networking, Google Cloud
- **Description:** New technologies and architectures for secure, high-performance multicloud/hybrid connectivity. Focus on **service-centric architectures** for AI agents and services.

### BRK3-039 — Improve AI developer velocity with an agentic cross-cloud network
- **Day:** April 24 (Day 3), 8:30–9:15 AM | Lagoon B
- **Level:** Advanced Technical | Networking
- **Description:** Introduces **new service-networking capabilities for GKE and Cloud Run** for agentic AI demands. Covers traffic management, **identity-based security**, real-time observability, and TCO reduction.

### BRK1-102 — What's new in network security
- **Day:** April 22 (Day 1), 12:30–1:15 PM | Breakers C
- **Level:** Introductory | Security
- **Description:** Complete wrap-up of **newest Cross-Cloud Network security features**. AI-powered capabilities for **Cloud NGFW**, **DNS Armor**, and **Google Cloud Armor**. New capability for **AI-assisted policy generation, recommendation, and simulation**.

### BRK3-048 — Best practices for designing and deploying Cross-Cloud Network security
- **Day:** April 22 (Day 1), 3:45–4:30 PM | Breakers C
- **Level:** Advanced Technical | Security
- **Description:** Expert guidance on **Cloud NGFW, Google Cloud Armor, and Secure Web Proxy** for protection against exploits, malware, DDoS, and data exfiltration.

### BRK3-048-DG — Simplify AI identity, governance and administration with agentic cross-cloud network
- **Day:** April 22 (Day 1), 5:15–5:45 PM | Reef F
- **Level:** Technical | Security (Discussion Group)
- **Description:** Covers **federated Agent Identities**, access authorizations, real-time protections. Open-source foundations for ISV ecosystem integration for AI safety and governance.

### BRK1-069-DG — AI inference: Performance when you need it, economy when you don't
- **Day:** April 23 (Day 2), 5:15–5:45 PM | Reef B
- **Level:** Advanced Technical | Networking (Discussion Group)
- **Description:** **GKE Inference Gateway innovations** — multi-accelerator flexibility, disaggregated serving for price-performance. Model Armor, agent identity, token-based quota protections. Optimization with accelerators across multiple cloud regions.

---

## 4. Anticipated Networking Infrastructure Announcements (Based on Session Descriptions & Pre-Event Signals)

### "Agentic Cross-Cloud Network" — The Central Theme
The dominant networking narrative at Next '26 is the evolution of Cross-Cloud Network into an **"agentic" platform**. Based on session descriptions:

1. **Centralized governance and observability** for connecting agents, models, and tools across clouds
2. **Federated Agent Identity** framework — treating AI agents like digital workers with strict security/compliance
3. **AI-assisted network security operations** — policy generation, recommendation, and simulation
4. **Service-networking for GKE and Cloud Run** — new capabilities purpose-built for agentic AI at high scale

### GKE Inference Gateway Expansions
- Multi-cluster Inference Gateway (announced preview March 17, 2026) — routes traffic across GKE clusters in different regions
- Cross-region accelerator pooling — eliminating compute silos
- Disaggregated serving and multi-accelerator flexibility
- Model Armor integration, agent identity, token-based quotas

### AI-Optimized Networking
- **AI-optimized VPC** — engineered to eliminate bottlenecks in massive model training and inference
- **AI-optimized GKE networking** — purpose-built for high-performance AI workloads

### Network Security Enhancements
- **Cloud NGFW URL filtering** (announced April 8, 2026) — domain-based filtering with wildcard capability, Layer 7 inspection
- **DNS Armor** updates (AI-powered, originally previewed at Next '25)
- **Google Cloud Armor** updates for AI-driven threat detection

### Data Center Infrastructure (from Chinese financial press)
- Rumors of **Optical Circuit Switch (OCS)** integration into data centers to optimize network latency and energy consumption
- Google reportedly needs ~15,000 300-port OCS switches in 2026 (~12,000 internal/Celestica, ~3,000 external)
- Possible **TPU v8 architecture** unveiling (next-generation custom AI silicon)

---

## 5. Context from Next '25 (Baseline for What's Being Built Upon)

At Next '25, Google Cloud Networking announced:
- **Cloud WAN** — fully managed global WAN, 40% TCO savings, 99.99% reliability
- **400G Cloud Interconnect & Cross-Cloud Interconnect** (4x bandwidth)
- **GKE Inference Gateway** (preview) — 30% cost reduction, 60% tail latency reduction
- **Support for 30,000 GPUs per cluster** in non-blocking configuration
- **3.2 Tbps GPU-to-GPU bandwidth** via RDMA
- **DNS Armor** (preview) — monitoring 70B DNS events daily
- **Cloud NGFW Enterprise** — 24x higher efficacy claimed
- **AWS–Google Cloud multicloud networking collaboration** (Azure expected 2026)
- **Cross-Site Interconnect** — first major cloud provider to offer dedicated point-to-point layer 2 private 10/100G connectivity

---

## 6. Key Speakers for Networking Track

| Speaker | Title | Sessions |
|---------|-------|----------|
| **Muninder Sambi** | VP & GM Networking | Cross-Cloud Infrastructure Spotlight |
| **Drew Bradstock** | Sr. Director, Compute & K8s Orchestration | Cross-Cloud Infrastructure Spotlight |
| **Himanshu Mehra** | Director PM, Networking | BRK3-038 (Cross-cloud network) |
| **Mark Lohmeyer** | VP & GM AI & Computing Infrastructure | Infrastructure Spotlight |
| **Thomas Kurian** | CEO, Google Cloud | Opening Keynote |

---

key_facts: Google Cloud Next '26 runs April 22–24, 2026 at Mandalay Bay Convention Center, Las Vegas; Partner Summit starts April 21
Opening keynote titled 'The Agentic Cloud' delivered by Thomas Kurian, CEO of Google Cloud, on Wednesday April 22 at 9:00 AM PT
Cross-Cloud Infrastructure Spotlight led by Muninder Sambi (VP & GM Networking) and Drew Bradstock (Sr. Director, Compute & K8s Orchestration) focuses on 'agentic cross-cloud infrastructure'
Infrastructure Spotlight led by Mark Lohmeyer (VP & GM AI & Computing Infrastructure) covers AI Hypercomputer and compute ecosystem
At least 7 confirmed networking-focused sessions in the session library, including breakouts and discussion groups
BRK3-037 introduces new Cross-Cloud Network capabilities: centralized governance, observability, GKE Inference Gateway expansions, and AI-optimized VPC
BRK3-039 introduces new service-networking capabilities for GKE and Cloud Run purpose-built for agentic AI demands
BRK1-102 covers newest Cross-Cloud Network security features including AI-assisted policy generation, recommendation, and simulation for Cloud NGFW, DNS Armor, and Cloud Armor
Multi-cluster GKE Inference Gateway entered preview on March 17, 2026, enabling cross-region traffic routing to accelerators
Cloud NGFW URL filtering with domain-based wildcard capability announced April 8, 2026
Discussion group BRK3-048-DG covers federated Agent Identities and access authorizations for AI workloads
Chinese financial press reports suggest potential TPU v8 architecture and ~15,000 Optical Circuit Switch (OCS) units planned for 2026 (~12,000 Celestica-manufactured, ~3,000 external)

Next '25 baseline: Cloud WAN launched (40% TCO savings), 400G interconnects, 3.2 Tbps RDMA, 30K GPU clusters, GKE Inference Gateway preview, DNS Armor preview, AWS–Google multicloud networking collaboration
Session library launched February 24, 2026; event expected to exceed Next '25 attendance record; ~30,000 estimated attendees; tickets start at $999
Four major conference themes: Agentic AI (ADK 2.0, A2A protocol), Gemini Enterprise upgrades, next-gen TPU architecture, and AI security/industry applications
